steps.title: Identify the energy ladder model's mechanism
steps.content: The passage defines the energy ladder model as one where “fuel choice is mediated mainly by household income (specifically, high-technology fuels displace solid fuels as incomes rise).” This is the central claim an advocate would use to explain any fuel shift.

steps.title: Apply the mechanism to Brazil's change
steps.content:
Choice A directly applies this logic: household incomes rose, enabling households to shift to high-technology fuels.

The other choices either cite a different causal factor—fuel availability (B) or relative pricing (C)—or contradict the model by suggesting income weakened its explanatory power (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
